---
title: "ADHDCYCLE_Single_GAMs_v2"
author: "Tory and Ashley"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---
Using individual GAM code from Tory - https://github.com/eisenlohrmoullab/hera/blob/main/HERA_08_single_GAMs.Rmd

# Libraries
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(mgcv)
library(patchwork)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)
library(tibble)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(broom)
library(scales)

options(scipen = 999)
set.seed(12345)
```

# Data
```{r}
cycle_df_scaled <- get(load("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/CYCLEADHD/03_analytic_projects/CYCADHD_PRIMARY/03_dataedits_output/0_dataprep_output/00_cleaned_datasets/adhd_daily_scaled_20250929.RData"))

```

# 1 - Set output folder & time vars to use
```{r}
# --- Path to the main output directory ---
gam_output_base <- "~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/CYCLEADHD/03_analytic_projects/CYCADHD_PRIMARY/03_dataedits_output/5b_Single_GAMs/"

# --- Define all Time Variables to iterate over (must be scaled to [-1, 1]) ---
time_vars_to_run <- c("cyclic_time_impute") #"cyclic_time", "cyclic_time_ov", "cyclic_time_imp_ov"

# --- Table Formatting ---
table_cols_per_page <- 6

# --- Plot Layout (grid per page) ---
plots_per_col <- 3
plots_per_row <- 3 
```

# 2 - Create output folders & check time vars
```{r}
# --- Create output folder with current date ---
current_date <- format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d")
output_folder <- file.path(gam_output_base, paste0("GAM_Reports_", current_date))
dir.create(output_folder, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# --- Robustness: Verify time variables exist ---
time_vars <- intersect(time_vars_to_run, names(cycle_df_scaled))
if (length(time_vars) < length(time_vars_to_run)) {
  missing_vars <- setdiff(time_vars_to_run, time_vars)
  warning("The following time_vars were not found and will be skipped: ", paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", "))
}
```

# 3 - Define outcomes
```{r}
outcomes <- c(
  "CSS_Inatt_Count" = "CSS Inattention Sx Count", "CSS_Hyp_Count" = "CSS Hyperactivity Sx Count",
  "CSS_Imp_Count" = "CSS Impulsivity Sx Count", "CSS_Inatt" = "CSS Inattention Sx Severity",
  "CSS_HypImp" = "CSS Hyperactivity/Impulsivity Sx Severity", "score_pinball" = "Working Memory Score (Pinball)",
  "score_robot" = "Response Inhibition (Robot Factory)", "BDEFS_WM_avg" = "Working Memory Sx (BDEFS)",
  "BDEFS_RI_avg" = "Response Inhibition Sx (BDEFS)", "DRSP_1" = "Depressed Mood", "DRSP_2" = "Hopelessness",
  "DRSP_3" = "Worthlessness/Guilt", "DRSP_4" = "Anxiety", "DRSP_5" = "Mood Swings",
  "DRSP_6" = "Rejection Sensitivity", "DRSP_7" = "Irritability", "DRSP_8" = "Interpersonal Conflicts",
  "DRSP_9" = "Less Interest in Activities", "DRSP_10" = "Difficulty Concentrating", "DRSP_11" = "Lethargy/Fatigue",
  "DRSP_12" = "Increased Appetite/Overate", "DRSP_13" = "Food Cravings", "DRSP_14" = "Hypersomnia",
  "DRSP_15" = "Insomnia", "DRSP_16" = "Overwhelmed/Couldn't Cope", "DRSP_17" = "Felt Out of Control",
  "DRSP_18" = "Breast Tenderness", "DRSP_19" = "Swelling/Bloating", "DRSP_20" = "Headache",
  "DRSP_21" = "Joint/Muscle Pain", "DRSP_22" = "Work Impairment", "DRSP_23" = "Relationship Impairment",
  "E2" = "Estradiol", "P4" = "Progesterone", "LH" = "Luteinizing Hormone"
)

# --- Define Categories for organization ---
outcome_categories <- tribble(
  ~category, ~variable,
  "ADHD Symptoms (CSS)", "CSS_Inatt_Count", "ADHD Symptoms (CSS)", "CSS_Hyp_Count",
  "ADHD Symptoms (CSS)", "CSS_Imp_Count", "ADHD Symptoms (CSS)", "CSS_Inatt",
  "ADHD Symptoms (CSS)", "CSS_HypImp",
  "Cognitive Score", "score_pinball", "Cognitive Score", "score_robot",
  "Executive Function (BDEFS)", "BDEFS_WM_avg", "Executive Function (BDEFS)", "BDEFS_RI_avg",
  "Hormone", "E2", "Hormone", "P4", "Hormone", "LH",
  "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_1", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_2", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_3",
  "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_4", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_5", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_6",
  "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_7", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_8", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_9",
  "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_10", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_11", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_12",
  "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_13", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_14", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_15",
  "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_16", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_17", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_18",
  "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_19", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_20", "Mood & Physical (DRSP)", "DRSP_21",
  "Impairment (DRSP)", "DRSP_22", "Impairment (DRSP)", "DRSP_23"
) %>%
  right_join(enframe(outcomes, name = "variable", value = "outcome_label"), by = "variable")

# --- Define custom page layouts ---
page_definitions <- c(
  list(
    CSS_Page = outcome_categories %>% filter(category == "ADHD Symptoms (CSS)") %>% pull(outcome_label),
    Hormone_Page = outcome_categories %>% filter(category == "Hormone") %>% pull(outcome_label),
    Cog_EF_Page = c("Response Inhibition (Robot Factory)", "Response Inhibition Sx (BDEFS)",
                    "Working Memory Score (Pinball)", "Working Memory Sx (BDEFS)")
  ),
  split(
    outcome_categories %>% filter(grepl("DRSP", category)) %>% pull(outcome_label),
    ceiling(seq_along(outcome_categories %>% filter(grepl("DRSP", category)) %>% pull(outcome_label)) / 9)
  )
)
names(page_definitions)[4:length(page_definitions)] <- paste0("DRSP_Page_", 1:(length(page_definitions)-3))

# --- Physical vs Affect symptom classification (raw DRSP items only) ---
physical_symptoms <- c("DRSP_18", "DRSP_19", "DRSP_20", "DRSP_21")

# --- Max possible counts per category (for density-plot Y-axis scaling) ---
all_raw_drsp <- names(outcomes)[grepl("^DRSP_\\d+$", names(outcomes))]
n_physical_max <- length(intersect(all_raw_drsp, physical_symptoms))
n_affect_max   <- length(setdiff(all_raw_drsp, physical_symptoms))

# --- Pre-calculate log-transformations ---
for (var in names(outcomes)) {
  if (var %in% names(cycle_df_scaled)) {
    log_var_name <- paste0(var, "_Log")
    cycle_df_scaled[[log_var_name]] <- log(cycle_df_scaled[[var]] + 1)
  }
}

# --- Define colors for plots (each outcome stays consistent) ---
## Use a colorblind friendly palette
drsp_color_map <- c(
  # DRSP mood/affect items
  "DRSP_1"  = "#0072B2",  # blue
  "DRSP_2"  = "#E69F00",  # orange
  "DRSP_3"  = "#009E73",  # green
  "DRSP_4"  = "#D55E00",  # vermillion
  "DRSP_5"  = "#CC79A7",  # reddish purple
  "DRSP_6"  = "#56B4E9",  # sky blue
  "DRSP_7"  = "#F0E442",  # yellow
  "DRSP_8"  = "#000000",  # black
  "DRSP_9"  = "#999999",  # gray
  "DRSP_10" = "#004488",  # dark blue
  "DRSP_11" = "#BB5566",  # muted red
  "DRSP_12" = "#228833",  # dark green
  "DRSP_13" = "#EE6677",  # soft red
  "DRSP_14" = "#AA3377",  # magenta
  "DRSP_15" = "#66CCEE",  # light cyan
  "DRSP_16" = "#332288",  # deep purple
  "DRSP_17" = "#117733",  # dark green 2
  # DRSP physical items
  "DRSP_18" = "#44AA99",  # teal
  "DRSP_19" = "#88CCEE",  # light blue
  "DRSP_20" = "#DDCC77",  # sand
  "DRSP_21" = "#CC6677",  # rose
  # DRSP impairment items
  "DRSP_22" = "#882255",  # dark rose
  "DRSP_23" = "#661100",  # dark red
  # CSS (ADHD symptoms)
  "CSS_Inatt_Count" = "#1B9E77",
  "CSS_Hyp_Count"   = "#D95F02",
  "CSS_Imp_Count"   = "#7570B3",
  "CSS_Inatt"       = "#E7298A",
  "CSS_HypImp"      = "#66A61E",
  # Cognitive scores
  "score_pinball" = "#E6AB02",
  "score_robot"   = "#A6761D",
  # BDEFS executive function
  "BDEFS_WM_avg" = "#666666",
  "BDEFS_RI_avg" = "#1F78B4",
  # Hormones
  "E2" = "#A6CEE3",
  "P4" = "#B2DF8A",
  "LH" = "#FB9A99"
)

# Safety check: warn (do not stop) if any outcome is missing a color
missing_colors <- setdiff(names(outcomes), names(drsp_color_map))
if (length(missing_colors) > 0) {
  warning("Missing colors for: ", paste(missing_colors, collapse = ", "),
          ". Falling back to #0072B2 for these outcomes.")
}

# --- Per-outcome y-axis limits and reference-line settings ---
# y_axis_mode "fixed" uses a hard y_min/y_max (Likert 1-6 scale).
# y_axis_mode "data"  uses data-driven limits; no fixed reference line.
outcome_plot_settings <- tribble(
  ~variable,          ~y_axis_mode, ~y_min, ~y_max, ~ref_line,
  "CSS_Inatt_Count",  "fixed",       0,      9,      NA,
  "CSS_Hyp_Count",    "fixed",       0,      5,      NA,
  "CSS_Imp_Count",    "fixed",       0,      4,      NA,
  "CSS_Inatt",        "fixed",       0,      3,      NA,
  "CSS_HypImp",       "fixed",       0,      3,      NA,
  "score_pinball",    "data",        NA,     NA,     NA,
  "score_robot",      "data",        NA,     NA,     NA,
  "BDEFS_WM_avg",     "fixed",        1,     4,     NA,
  "BDEFS_RI_avg",     "fixed",        1,     4,     NA,
  "DRSP_1",           "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_2",           "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_3",           "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_4",           "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_5",           "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_6",           "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_7",           "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_8",           "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_9",           "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_10",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_11",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_12",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_13",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_14",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_15",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_16",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_17",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_18",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_19",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_20",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_21",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_22",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "DRSP_23",          "fixed",       1,      6,      4,
  "E2",               "data",        NA,     NA,     NA,
  "P4",               "data",        NA,     NA,     NA,
  "LH",               "data",        NA,     NA,     NA
)
```

# 4 - Per ID outcomes
```{r}
# --- Prediction grids reused per time var ---
pred_grid_list <- setNames(lapply(time_vars, function(tv) {
  pg_df <- data.frame(seq(-1, 1, by = 0.05))
  names(pg_df) <- tv
  pg_df
}), time_vars)

# --- Axis labels + reference lines per time var ---
axis_meta <- lapply(time_vars, function(tv) {
  if (grepl("ov", tv, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    list(labels = c("Menses", "50%F", "Ovulation", "50%L", "Menses"),
         menses = c(-1, 1), ov = 0)
  } else {
    list(labels = c("Ovulation", "50%L", "Menses", "50%F", "Ovulation"),
         menses = 0, ov = c(-1, 1))
  }
})
names(axis_meta) <- time_vars

# --- Fit + predict one outcome for one time var (bam + predict) ---
fit_outcome <- function(dat, outcome_var, tv, outcomes_map, k_max = 10L) {
  log_out <- paste0(outcome_var, "_Log")
  keep <- c(log_out, tv)
  if (!all(keep %in% names(dat))) return(NULL)
  dat <- dat[complete.cases(dat[, keep]), keep, drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(dat) < 10 || var(dat[[log_out]], na.rm = TRUE) < 1e-6) return(NULL) #removes outcomes with <10 observations or outcome has 0 variance

  # Count observations in each phase half for adequacy flagging
  n_pre  <- sum(dat[[tv]] <  0, na.rm = TRUE)
  n_post <- sum(dat[[tv]] >= 0, na.rm = TRUE)

  k <- min(k_max, nrow(dat) - 1L); if (k < 3L) return(NULL)

  fml <- as.formula(paste0(log_out, " ~ s(", tv, ", bs = 'cc', k = ", k, ")"))
  knots <- setNames(list(c(-1, 1)), tv)

  mf <- try(mgcv::bam(fml, data = dat, knots = knots, method = "fREML",
                      discrete = TRUE, select = TRUE),
            silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(mf, "try-error")) return(NULL)

  s  <- summary(mf)
  p  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(s$s.pv[1]))
  st <- s$s.table
  edf <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(st[1, "edf"]))
  Fval <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(st[1, "F"]))

  pg <- pred_grid_list[[tv]]
  pr <- predict(mf, newdata = pg, se.fit = TRUE, type = "link")  # link scale for log(y+1)
  est <- exp(pr$fit) - 1
  conf.low  <- exp(pr$fit - 1.96 * pr$se.fit) - 1
  conf.high <- exp(pr$fit + 1.96 * pr$se.fit) - 1

  # Record cycle time at peak (max) and trough (min) of predicted curve
  # pg and est are aligned: pg is the prediction grid, est is the back-transformed prediction
  peak_time   <- pg[[tv]][which.max(est)]
  trough_time <- pg[[tv]][which.min(est)]

  pred <- cbind(pg, estimate = est, conf.low = conf.low, conf.high = conf.high)
  #pred$outcome_label <- outcomes_map[[outcome_var]]
  pred$outcome_var <- outcome_var                    # AR - update 4/4/26
  pred$outcome_label <- outcomes_map[[outcome_var]]  # AR - update 4/4/26
  pred$p.value <- p
  pred$raw_range <- diff(range(est, na.rm = TRUE))
  pred$n <- nrow(dat)

  list(
    pred = pred,
    sig  = tibble::tibble(
      variable = outcome_var,
      outcome_label = outcomes_map[[outcome_var]],
      time_var = tv,
      p.value = p,
      edf = edf,
      F = Fval,
      n = nrow(dat),
      n_pre = n_pre,
      n_post = n_post,
      range = diff(range(est, na.rm = TRUE)),
      peak_time = peak_time,
      trough_time = trough_time
    )
  )
}

# --- Plot one outcome curve with annotations ---
# y_limits = NULL means: use per-outcome defaults from outcome_plot_settings
#   (fixed outcomes → c(y_min, y_max); data-mode outcomes → data-driven, no clamp)
# y_limits = c(lo, hi) means: use those limits as-is (e.g. from shared-y section)
create_annotated_plot <- function(plot_df, tv, axis_meta_list, y_limits = NULL) {
  if (is.null(plot_df) || nrow(plot_df) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  max_pt <- dplyr::slice_max(plot_df, order_by = estimate, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
  min_pt <- dplyr::slice_min(plot_df, order_by = estimate, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
  rng   <- dplyr::first(plot_df$raw_range)
  p_val <- dplyr::first(plot_df$p.value)
  n_obs <- dplyr::first(plot_df$n)
  
  outcome_var <- dplyr::first(plot_df$outcome_var)
  line_col <- drsp_color_map[[outcome_var]]
  if (is.null(line_col) || is.na(line_col)) line_col <- "#0072B2"

  # Look up per-outcome y-axis and reference-line settings
  ps        <- outcome_plot_settings[outcome_plot_settings$variable == outcome_var, ]
  mode      <- if (nrow(ps) > 0) ps$y_axis_mode[1] else "fixed"
  ref_line  <- if (nrow(ps) > 0) ps$ref_line[1]    else 4
  y_fix_min <- if (nrow(ps) > 0) ps$y_min[1]       else 1
  y_fix_max <- if (nrow(ps) > 0) ps$y_max[1]       else 6

  # Resolve y-axis limits when not explicitly supplied by the caller
  if (is.null(y_limits)) {
    if (mode == "fixed" && !is.na(y_fix_min) && !is.na(y_fix_max)) {
      y_limits <- c(y_fix_min, y_fix_max)
    }
    # data mode: leave y_limits = NULL → ggplot auto-scales
  }
  
  range_band_label <- dplyr::case_when(
    is.finite(rng) && rng >= 1.5 ~ "Range >= 1.5 | ",
    is.finite(rng) && rng >= 1.0 ~ "Range 1.0-1.49 | ",
    is.finite(rng) && rng >= 0.5 ~ "Range 0.5-0.99 | ",
    TRUE                         ~ ""
  )
  
  stars <- dplyr::case_when(
    is.finite(p_val) & p_val < 0.001 ~ "***",
    is.finite(p_val) & p_val < 0.01  ~ "**",
    is.finite(p_val) & p_val < 0.05  ~ "*",
    TRUE ~ ""
  )
  
  ann <- if (!is.null(n_obs) && !is.na(n_obs)) {
    sprintf("%s%s Range = %.2f, N = %d", range_band_label, stars, rng, as.integer(n_obs))
  } else {
    sprintf("%s%s Range = %.2f", range_band_label, stars, rng)
  }
  
  meta <- axis_meta_list[[tv]]

  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(plot_df, ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[tv]], y = estimate))

  # Reference line only for outcomes that define one (Likert-scale outcomes)
  if (!is.na(ref_line)) {
    p <- p + ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = ref_line, color = "red",
                                 linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.4)
  }

  p <- p +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$menses, color = "red") +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$ov, color = "forestgreen") +
    ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = line_col) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = max_pt, size = 2.5, color = line_col) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = min_pt, size = 2.5, color = line_col) +
    ggplot2::annotate("text", x = -1, y = Inf, label = ann,
                      hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1.5, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
    ggplot2::labs(title = dplyr::first(plot_df$outcome_label), x = "Cycle Phase", y = NULL) +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(
      limits = c(-1, 1),
      breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.5),
      labels = meta$labels
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    )

  # Apply y-axis limits only when defined (NULL → ggplot auto-scales)
  if (!is.null(y_limits)) {
    p <- p + ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = y_limits)
  }

  border_col <- dplyr::case_when(
    is.finite(rng) && rng >= 1.5 ~ "red",
    is.finite(rng) && rng >= 1.0 ~ "salmon",
    is.finite(rng) && rng >= 0.5 ~ "pink",
    TRUE                         ~ NA_character_
  )
  
  if (!is.na(border_col)) {
    p <- p + ggplot2::theme(
      panel.border = ggplot2::element_rect(color = border_col, linewidth = 1.5, fill = NA)
    )
  }

  p
}

# --- Compute shared per-ID y-limits for a plot, clamped to [1, 6] ---
make_shared_id_y_limits <- function(plot_df, shared_total_range, lower_bound = 1, upper_bound = 6) {
  this_ymin <- min(plot_df$estimate, na.rm = TRUE)
  this_ymax <- max(plot_df$estimate, na.rm = TRUE)
  this_mid  <- (this_ymin + this_ymax) / 2
  
  half_span <- shared_total_range / 2
  y_low  <- this_mid - half_span
  y_high <- this_mid + half_span
  
  # Clamp to allowed symptom scale
  if (y_low < lower_bound) {
    shift_up <- lower_bound - y_low
    y_low  <- y_low + shift_up
    y_high <- y_high + shift_up
  }
  if (y_high > upper_bound) {
    shift_down <- y_high - upper_bound
    y_low  <- y_low - shift_down
    y_high <- y_high - shift_down
  }
  
  # Final safeguard in case shared_total_range is very large
  y_low  <- max(y_low, lower_bound)
  y_high <- min(y_high, upper_bound)
  
  c(y_low, y_high)
}
```

# 5 - Per ID outcomes reports
```{r}
report_folder <- file.path(output_folder, "Participant_Reports_PDF_byID")
dir.create(report_folder, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

ids_to_run    <- unique(cycle_df_scaled$id)   # set to subset for testing, e.g., c("203","109")
skip_existing <- TRUE            # set FALSE to overwrite

# collector for a single CSV at the end
all_ids_signif <- list()

# fixed layout controls
max_plots_per_page <- plots_per_col * plots_per_row
table_theme <- gridExtra::ttheme_default(
  base_size = 9,
  core   = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0, x = 0.05)),
  colhead = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0, x = 0.05))
)

for (current_id in ids_to_run) {
  message(sprintf("--- Processing ID: %s ---", current_id))
  datSX_id <- subset(cycle_df_scaled, id == current_id)

  # Pass 1: fit & collect
  preds_by_tv <- list()
  sig_by_tv   <- list()

  for (tv in time_vars) {
    message(sprintf("  ... time variable: %s", tv))
    all_preds <- list()
    sig_list  <- list()

    for (outcome_var in names(outcomes)) {
      res <- fit_outcome(datSX_id, outcome_var, tv, outcomes)
      if (is.null(res)) next
      all_preds[[length(all_preds) + 1]] <- res$pred
      sig_list[[length(sig_list) + 1]]  <- res$sig
    }

    if (length(all_preds) > 0) {
      preds_by_tv[[tv]] <- dplyr::bind_rows(all_preds)
      sig_by_tv[[tv]]   <- dplyr::bind_rows(sig_list)
    }
  }

  if (!length(preds_by_tv)) {
    message("    -> No successful models for this ID; skipping.")
    next
  }

  # Build per-ID significance object for CSV
  sig_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(sig_by_tv)
  sig_all$id <- current_id
  all_ids_signif[[as.character(current_id)]] <- sig_all

  # ---- Open a single LANDSCAPE PDF per participant ----
  pdf_filename <- file.path(report_folder, sprintf("Report_ID_%s.pdf", current_id))
  if (isTRUE(skip_existing) && file.exists(pdf_filename)) {
    message("    -> File exists; skipping. Set skip_existing=FALSE to overwrite.")
    next
  }
  grDevices::pdf(pdf_filename, width = 11, height = 8.5)   # LANDSCAPE

  # ---- COVER: Compact counts by category x time_var ----
  sig_all_clean <- sig_all %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      stars = dplyr::case_when(
        is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
        is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(dplyr::select(outcome_categories, variable, category), by = "variable")

  cover_cpass <- sig_all_clean %>%
    dplyr::group_by(category) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      `Range ≥1.5 sig` = sum(is.finite(range) & range >= 1.5 & stars != "", na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  cover_counts <- sig_all_clean %>%
    dplyr::group_by(category, time_var) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      tested = dplyr::n(),
      n_sig  = sum(stars != "", na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    tidyr::unite("count", n_sig, tested, sep = " / ") %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = time_var, values_from = count, values_fill = "0 / 0") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(cover_cpass, by = "category") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(category)

  cover_theme <- gridExtra::ttheme_default(
    base_size = 9,
    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5, fontface = "bold"),
      padding  = grid::unit(c(2, 2), "mm")
    ),
    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5, fontface = "bold")
    )
  )

  title_grob    <- grid::textGrob(paste("Compact Summary — ID:", current_id),
                                  gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 16, fontface = "bold"))
  subtitle_grob <- grid::textGrob("Cells show: # significant / # tested (p < .05) per category × time variable",
                                  gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9))
  cover_grob    <- gridExtra::tableGrob(cover_counts, rows = NULL, theme = cover_theme)
  gridExtra::grid.arrange(title_grob, subtitle_grob, cover_grob,
                          ncol = 1, heights = grid::unit(c(0.12, 0.08, 0.80), "npc"))

  # ---- OPTIONAL: compact stars matrix for outcomes with any significance ----
  compact_stars <- sig_all_clean %>%
    dplyr::group_by(variable) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(any_sig = any(is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::filter(any_sig) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Outcome = dplyr::coalesce(outcome_label, variable)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Outcome = ifelse(nchar(Outcome) > 22, variable, Outcome),
      stars = dplyr::case_when(
        is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
        is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(category, Outcome, time_var, stars) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = time_var, values_from = stars, values_fill = "") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(category, Outcome)

  if (nrow(compact_stars) > 0) {
    compact_theme <- gridExtra::ttheme_default(
      base_size = 8,
      core = list(
        fg_params = list(hjust = 0, x = 0.02),
        padding = grid::unit(c(1.5, 2), "mm")
      ),
      colhead = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5, fontface = "bold"))
    )
    header <- grid::textGrob("Significant Outcomes Only (★ = p < .05)",
                             gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold"))
    tbl <- gridExtra::tableGrob(compact_stars, rows = NULL, theme = compact_theme)
    gridExtra::grid.arrange(header, tbl, ncol = 1, heights = grid::unit(c(0.10, 0.90), "npc"))
  }
  
  # ---- PAGE 2: Significant EDFs ranked by Range (across all time vars) ----
sig_ranked <- sig_all_clean %>%
  dplyr::filter(is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.05) %>%     # keep significant smooths
  dplyr::mutate(
    Outcome = dplyr::coalesce(outcome_label, variable),
    Outcome = ifelse(nchar(Outcome) > 40, variable, Outcome) # shorten long names
  ) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    Category = category,
    Outcome,
    TimeVar  = time_var,
    EDF      = round(edf, 2),
    Range    = round(range, 2),
    F        = round(F, 2),
    N        = n
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(Range), Category, Outcome, TimeVar)

if (nrow(sig_ranked) == 0) {
  # show an empty-state page
  header <- grid::textGrob(
    paste("Significant Smooths — ID:", current_id, "(none at p < .05)"),
    gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold")
  )
  sub <- grid::textGrob("No significant cyclic effects detected across any time variable.",
                        gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "italic"))
  gridExtra::grid.arrange(header, sub, ncol = 1,
                          heights = grid::unit(c(0.2, 0.8), "npc"))
} else {
  # compact table theme to fit on a single landscape page
  ranked_theme <- gridExtra::ttheme_default(
    base_size = 8,
    core = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0, x = 0.02),
      padding = grid::unit(c(1.2, 2), "mm")
    ),
    colhead = list(
      fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5, fontface = "bold")
    )
  )
  title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
    paste("Significant Smooths (p < .05), Ranked by Range — ID:", current_id),
    gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold")
  )
  note_grob <- grid::textGrob(
    "Columns: EDF = effective degrees of freedom; Range = max(pred) − min(pred) on original scale.",
    gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9, fontface = "italic")
  )
  ranked_tbl <- gridExtra::tableGrob(sig_ranked, rows = NULL, theme = ranked_theme)

  gridExtra::grid.arrange(
    title_grob, note_grob, ranked_tbl,
    ncol = 1,
    heights = grid::unit(c(0.12, 0.06, 0.82), "npc")
  )
}

  # ---- Person-Specific Peak & Trough Timing Distribution ----
  # Separate density plots for Physical vs Affect symptoms
  # Only raw DRSP items with p < .05 or range >= 1.5
  for (tv in names(sig_by_tv)) {
    sig_tv <- sig_by_tv[[tv]]
    if (nrow(sig_tv) == 0) next

    # Keep only raw DRSP items (drsp_1, drsp_2, …), exclude composites;
    # further restrict to items that are significant (p < .05) OR have range >= 1.5
    sig_tv_raw <- sig_tv %>%
      dplyr::filter(grepl("^DRSP_\\d+$", variable)) %>%
      dplyr::filter(
        (is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.05) |
        (is.finite(range) & range >= 1.5)
      )
    if (nrow(sig_tv_raw) == 0) next

    meta <- axis_meta[[tv]]

  # Helper to build a peak/trough timing plot for one symptom category.
# Uses a histogram-style count (binwidth = 0.1 on the [-1,1] scale) so the
# y-axis literally = number of qualifying items whose peak/trough falls in
# that bin. max_n fixes the axis ceiling = total possible qualifying items,
# making plots comparable across participants.
make_timing_density <- function(sig_subset, category_label, max_n) {
  tl <- dplyr::bind_rows(
    sig_subset %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(peak_time)) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(time = peak_time,   type = "Peak (Max)",   outcome = outcome_label),
    sig_subset %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(trough_time)) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(time = trough_time, type = "Trough (Min)", outcome = outcome_label)
  )
  if (nrow(tl) < 2) return(NULL)

  tl_peak   <- dplyr::filter(tl, type == "Peak (Max)")
  tl_trough <- dplyr::filter(tl, type == "Trough (Min)")

  # Need at least 1 in each group to plot
  if (nrow(tl_peak) < 1 || nrow(tl_trough) < 1) return(NULL)

  # --- Bin the peak/trough times into counts on a fixed grid ---
  bins   <- seq(-1, 1, by = 0.1)
  bin_mid <- bins[-length(bins)] + 0.05  # midpoints

  count_bins <- function(times) {
    counts <- hist(times, breaks = bins, plot = FALSE)$counts
    data.frame(x = bin_mid, count = counts)
  }

  df_peak   <- count_bins(tl_peak$time)
  df_trough <- count_bins(tl_trough$time)
  df_trough$count <- -df_trough$count  # mirror downward for troughs

  ggplot2::ggplot() +
    # Peaks upward (orange)
    ggplot2::geom_col(data = df_peak,
                      ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = count),
                      width = 0.09, fill = "#D55E00", color = "#D55E00", alpha = 0.6) +
    # Troughs downward (blue, already negative)
    ggplot2::geom_col(data = df_trough,
                      ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = count),
                      width = 0.09, fill = "#0072B2", color = "#0072B2", alpha = 0.6) +
    # Rug marks showing exact peak/trough locations
    ggplot2::geom_rug(data = tl_peak,
                      ggplot2::aes(x = time), color = "#D55E00",
                      alpha = 0.7, length = grid::unit(0.03, "npc"), sides = "b") +
    ggplot2::geom_rug(data = tl_trough,
                      ggplot2::aes(x = time), color = "#0072B2",
                      alpha = 0.7, length = grid::unit(0.03, "npc"), sides = "t") +
    ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "grey40") +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$menses, color = "red",
                        linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$ov, color = "forestgreen",
                        linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1),
                                breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.5),
                                labels = meta$labels) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-max_n, max_n),
                                breaks = seq(-max_n, max_n, by = 1),
                                labels = abs) +
    ggplot2::annotate("text", x = -0.95, y =  max_n * 0.9,
                      label = paste0("Peaks (orange): n = ", nrow(tl_peak)),
                      hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = "#D55E00", fontface = "bold") +
    ggplot2::annotate("text", x = -0.95, y = -max_n * 0.9,
                      label = paste0("Troughs (blue): n = ", nrow(tl_trough)),
                      hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = "#0072B2", fontface = "bold") +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title    = paste0(category_label, " Symptoms \u2014 ID: ", current_id),
      subtitle = paste0("Items with p < .05 or range \u2265 1.5  |  ",
                        "N qualifying = ", nrow(sig_subset), " / ", max_n,
                        "  |  Time variable: ", tv,
                        "\nBars = count of items peaking/troughing in each 0.1-unit bin"),
      x = "Cycle Phase",
      y = paste0("N items: Peaks \u2191 / Troughs \u2193 (max = ", max_n, ")")
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
}

    p_physical <- make_timing_density(
      sig_tv_raw %>% dplyr::filter(variable %in% physical_symptoms), "Physical",
      n_physical_max
    )
    p_affect <- make_timing_density(
      sig_tv_raw %>% dplyr::filter(!variable %in% physical_symptoms), "Affect",
      n_affect_max
    )

    # Print each plot on its own page
    if (!is.null(p_physical)) print(p_physical)
    if (!is.null(p_affect))   print(p_affect)
  }

  # ---- Overlay page: all qualifying individual GAM curves on one plot ----
  # Show individual DRSP items (not composites) that are significant (p < .05) OR have range >= 1.5
  for (tv in names(preds_by_tv)) {
    sig_tv   <- sig_by_tv[[tv]]
    pred_tv  <- preds_by_tv[[tv]]
    meta     <- axis_meta[[tv]]

    # Identify qualifying outcomes (individual DRSP items only, exclude composites)
    qualifying <- sig_tv %>%
      dplyr::filter(grepl("^DRSP_\\d+$", variable)) %>%
      dplyr::filter(
        (is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.05) |
        (is.finite(range) & range >= 1.5)
      )
    if (nrow(qualifying) == 0) next
 
    # AR edits to color mapping 4/4/26
    
    overlay_vars <- unique(qualifying$variable)
    overlay_data <- pred_tv %>%
    dplyr::filter(outcome_var %in% overlay_vars)
    if (nrow(overlay_data) == 0) next

    n_curves <- length(overlay_vars)

    overlay_colors <- drsp_color_map[overlay_vars]
    overlay_colors[is.na(overlay_colors)] <- "#0072B2"
    names(overlay_colors) <- overlay_vars

    p_overlay <- ggplot2::ggplot(overlay_data,
    ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[tv]], y = estimate, color = outcome_var)) +
    ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 4, color = "red",
                      linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.4) +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$menses, color = "red",
                      linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$ov, color = "forestgreen",
                      linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +
    ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
      values = overlay_colors,
      breaks = overlay_vars,
      labels = outcomes[overlay_vars]
      ) +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1),
                              breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.5),
                              labels = meta$labels) +
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1, 6)) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste0("Overlaid GAM Curves \u2014 ID: ", current_id),
      subtitle = paste0("Individual items with p < .05 or range \u2265 1.5  |  ",
                      "N curves = ", n_curves, "  |  Time variable: ", tv),
      x = "Cycle Phase", y = "Predicted Score",
      color = "Outcome"
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 7),
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
    ) +
    ggplot2::guides(
      color = ggplot2::guide_legend(ncol = 3)
    )

  print(p_overlay)
  }

  # ---- Overlay page: all qualifying individual GAM curves on one plot ----
  # Show individual DRSP items (not composites) that are significant (p < .05) OR have range >= 1.0
  for (tv in names(preds_by_tv)) {
    sig_tv   <- sig_by_tv[[tv]]
    pred_tv  <- preds_by_tv[[tv]]
    meta     <- axis_meta[[tv]]

    # Identify qualifying outcomes (individual DRSP items only, exclude composites)
    qualifying_1 <- sig_tv %>%
      dplyr::filter(grepl("^DRSP_\\d+$", variable)) %>%
      dplyr::filter(
        (is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.05) |
        (is.finite(range) & range >= 1.0)
      )
    if (nrow(qualifying_1) == 0) next

    overlay_vars_1 <- unique(qualifying_1$variable)
    overlay_data_1 <- pred_tv %>%
      dplyr::filter(outcome_var %in% overlay_vars_1)
    if (nrow(overlay_data_1) == 0) next

    n_curves_1 <- length(overlay_vars_1)

    overlay_colors_1 <- drsp_color_map[overlay_vars_1]
    overlay_colors_1[is.na(overlay_colors_1)] <- "#0072B2"
    names(overlay_colors_1) <- overlay_vars_1

    p_overlay_1 <- ggplot2::ggplot(
      overlay_data_1,
      ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[tv]], y = estimate, color = outcome_var)
    ) +
      ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 4, color = "red",
                          linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.4) +
      ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$menses, color = "red",
                          linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +
      ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$ov, color = "forestgreen",
                          linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +
      ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
      ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
        values = overlay_colors_1,
        breaks = overlay_vars_1,
        labels = outcomes[overlay_vars_1]
      ) +
      ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(
        limits = c(-1, 1),
        breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.5),
        labels = meta$labels
      ) +
      ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1, 6)) +
      ggplot2::labs(
        title = paste0("Overlaid GAM Curves \u2014 ID: ", current_id),
        subtitle = paste0(
          "Individual items with p < .05 or range \u2265 1.0  |  ",
          "N curves = ", n_curves_1, "  |  Time variable: ", tv
        ),
        x = "Cycle Phase",
        y = "Predicted Score",
        color = "Outcome"
      ) +
      ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      ggplot2::theme(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 7),
        plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
      ) +
      ggplot2::guides(
        color = ggplot2::guide_legend(ncol = 3)
      )

    print(p_overlay_1)
  }
  
  # ---- Overlay page: all qualifying individual GAM curves on one plot ----
  # Show individual DRSP items (not composites) that are significant (p < .05) OR have range >= 0.5
  for (tv in names(preds_by_tv)) {
    sig_tv   <- sig_by_tv[[tv]]
    pred_tv  <- preds_by_tv[[tv]]
    meta     <- axis_meta[[tv]]

    # Identify qualifying outcomes (individual DRSP items only, exclude composites)
    qualifying_05 <- sig_tv %>%
      dplyr::filter(grepl("^DRSP_\\d+$", variable)) %>%
      dplyr::filter(
        (is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.05) |
        (is.finite(range) & range >= 0.5)
      )
    if (nrow(qualifying_05) == 0) next

    overlay_vars_05 <- unique(qualifying_05$variable)
    overlay_data_05 <- pred_tv %>%
      dplyr::filter(outcome_var %in% overlay_vars_05)
    if (nrow(overlay_data_05) == 0) next

    n_curves_05 <- length(overlay_vars_05)

    overlay_colors_05 <- drsp_color_map[overlay_vars_05]
    overlay_colors_05[is.na(overlay_colors_05)] <- "#0072B2"
    names(overlay_colors_05) <- overlay_vars_05

    p_overlay_05 <- ggplot2::ggplot(
      overlay_data_05,
      ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[tv]], y = estimate, color = outcome_var)
    ) +
      ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 4, color = "red",
                          linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.4) +
      ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$menses, color = "red",
                          linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +
      ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$ov, color = "forestgreen",
                          linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +
      ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
      ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
        values = overlay_colors_05,
        breaks = overlay_vars_05,
        labels = outcomes[overlay_vars_05]
      ) +
      ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(
        limits = c(-1, 1),
        breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.5),
        labels = meta$labels
      ) +
      ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1, 6)) +
      ggplot2::labs(
        title = paste0("Overlaid GAM Curves \u2014 ID: ", current_id),
        subtitle = paste0(
          "Individual items with p < .05 or range \u2265 0.5  |  ",
          "N curves = ", n_curves_05, "  |  Time variable: ", tv
        ),
        x = "Cycle Phase",
        y = "Predicted Score",
        color = "Outcome"
      ) +
      ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      ggplot2::theme(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 7),
        plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
      ) +
      ggplot2::guides(
        color = ggplot2::guide_legend(ncol = 3)
      )

    print(p_overlay_05)
  }
  
  # ---- Sections per time variable: compact table + pages of plots ----
  for (tv in names(preds_by_tv)) {

    # NA-safe compact significance table (optionally filter to significant only)
    summary_df <- sig_by_tv[[tv]] %>%
      dplyr::left_join(dplyr::select(outcome_categories, variable, category), by = "variable") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        stars = dplyr::case_when(
          is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
          is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
          is.finite(p.value) & p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
          TRUE ~ ""
        ),
        Outcome = dplyr::coalesce(outcome_label, variable)
      ) %>%
      dplyr::select(Category = category, Outcome, N = n, EDF = edf, F = F, Range = range, Sig = stars) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(Category, Outcome)
    # Uncomment to show significant rows only:
    # summary_df <- dplyr::filter(summary_df, Sig != "")

    tv_title <- grid::textGrob(paste("Significance — ID:", current_id, "| Time Variable:", tv),
                               gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))
    tv_table <- gridExtra::tableGrob(summary_df, rows = NULL, theme = table_theme)
    gridExtra::grid.arrange(tv_title, tv_table, ncol = 1,
                            heights = grid::unit(c(0.12, 0.88), "npc"))

    # Plots by predefined pages
    combined_plot_data <- preds_by_tv[[tv]]
    for (page_name in names(page_definitions)) {
      page_outcomes <- page_definitions[[page_name]]
      page_data <- dplyr::filter(combined_plot_data, outcome_label %in% page_outcomes)
      if (nrow(page_data) == 0) next

      plot_list <- lapply(page_outcomes, function(lab) {
        plot_df <- dplyr::filter(page_data, outcome_label == lab)
        create_annotated_plot(plot_df, tv, axis_meta)
      })
      plot_list <- Filter(Negate(is.null), plot_list)

      if (length(plot_list) > 0) {
        if (length(plot_list) < max_plots_per_page) {
          plot_list <- c(plot_list,
                         rep(list(patchwork::plot_spacer()), max_plots_per_page - length(plot_list)))
        }
        page_title <- paste("Symptom & Hormone Trajectories — ID:", current_id, "| Time Variable:", tv)
        final_page <- patchwork::wrap_plots(plotlist = plot_list, ncol = plots_per_col) +
          patchwork::plot_annotation(
            title = page_title,
            theme = ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))
          )
        print(final_page)
      }
    }
  }

    # ---- NEW PAGE SET: Symptom trajectories with ID-specific shared y-range ----
  # For each ID and time variable:
  #   - find the largest predicted range per y_axis_mode group (fixed / data)
  #   - add 0.25, then apply that shared total y-range to every outcome in the group
  #   - fixed-scale outcomes (DRSP/CSS): clamped to [1, 6]
  #   - data-scale outcomes (hormones/scores): unclamped, data-driven bounds
  for (tv in names(preds_by_tv)) {

    combined_plot_data <- preds_by_tv[[tv]]
    if (is.null(combined_plot_data) || nrow(combined_plot_data) == 0) next

    # Compute outcome-specific predicted ranges and join with plot settings
    id_range_df <- combined_plot_data %>%
      dplyr::group_by(outcome_var, outcome_label) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(
        ymin = min(estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
        ymax = max(estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
        yrange = ymax - ymin,
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      dplyr::left_join(
        dplyr::select(outcome_plot_settings, variable, y_axis_mode, y_min, y_max),
        by = c("outcome_var" = "variable")
      ) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        y_axis_mode = dplyr::coalesce(y_axis_mode, "fixed"),
        # Fixed outcomes: clamp to configured scale bounds; data outcomes: use data bounds
        lower_bound = dplyr::if_else(
          y_axis_mode == "fixed", dplyr::coalesce(y_min, 1), ymin - 0.5
        ),
        upper_bound = dplyr::if_else(
          y_axis_mode == "fixed", dplyr::coalesce(y_max, 6), ymax + 0.5
        )
      )

    if (nrow(id_range_df) == 0) next

    # Shared total range computed WITHIN each y_axis_mode group
    shared_range_by_mode <- id_range_df %>%
      dplyr::group_by(y_axis_mode) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(
        shared_total_range = max(yrange, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.25,
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      # Cap fixed-scale shared range to the maximum scale span (e.g. 5 for 1-6)
      dplyr::mutate(
        shared_total_range = dplyr::if_else(
          y_axis_mode == "fixed",
          pmin(shared_total_range, 5),
          shared_total_range
        )
      )

    all_outcome_labels <- unique(combined_plot_data$outcome_label)
    outcome_pages_shared <- split(
      all_outcome_labels,
      ceiling(seq_along(all_outcome_labels) / max_plots_per_page)
    )

    for (page_outcomes in outcome_pages_shared) {
      page_data <- dplyr::filter(combined_plot_data, outcome_label %in% page_outcomes)
      if (nrow(page_data) == 0) next

      plot_list_shared <- lapply(page_outcomes, function(lab) {
        plot_df <- dplyr::filter(page_data, outcome_label == lab)
        if (nrow(plot_df) == 0) return(NULL)

        ov        <- dplyr::first(plot_df$outcome_var)
        mode_row  <- id_range_df[id_range_df$outcome_var == ov, ]
        mode_str  <- if (nrow(mode_row) > 0) mode_row$y_axis_mode[1] else "fixed"
        lb        <- if (nrow(mode_row) > 0) mode_row$lower_bound[1] else 1
        ub        <- if (nrow(mode_row) > 0) mode_row$upper_bound[1] else 6
        rng_row   <- shared_range_by_mode[shared_range_by_mode$y_axis_mode == mode_str, ]
        str_range <- if (nrow(rng_row) > 0) rng_row$shared_total_range[1] else 5

        shared_y_limits <- make_shared_id_y_limits(
          plot_df = plot_df,
          shared_total_range = str_range,
          lower_bound = lb,
          upper_bound = ub
        )

        create_annotated_plot(
          plot_df = plot_df,
          tv = tv,
          axis_meta_list = axis_meta,
          y_limits = shared_y_limits
        )
      })

      plot_list_shared <- Filter(Negate(is.null), plot_list_shared)

      if (length(plot_list_shared) > 0) {
        if (length(plot_list_shared) < max_plots_per_page) {
          plot_list_shared <- c(
            plot_list_shared,
            rep(list(patchwork::plot_spacer()), max_plots_per_page - length(plot_list_shared))
          )
        }

        page_title_shared <- paste(
          "Symptom Trajectories (ID-Scaled Y Axis) — ID:", current_id,
          "| Time Variable:", tv
        )

        page_subtitle_shared <- paste0(
          "Shared y-range per scale group (fixed=Likert [1-6]; data=data-driven). ",
          "Time variable: ", tv
        )

        final_page_shared <- patchwork::wrap_plots(
          plotlist = plot_list_shared,
          ncol = plots_per_col
        ) +
          patchwork::plot_annotation(
            title = page_title_shared,
            subtitle = page_subtitle_shared,
            theme = ggplot2::theme(
              plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
              plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11)
            )
          )

        print(final_page_shared)
      }
    }
  }
  
  grDevices::dev.off()
  message(sprintf("    --- ✅ Saved Report: %s ---", pdf_filename))
}

# ---- Write long CSV for evaluation across IDs ----
if (length(all_ids_signif)) {
  eval_csv <- file.path(output_folder, paste0("participant_significance_summary_", current_date, ".csv"))
  write.csv(dplyr::bind_rows(all_ids_signif), eval_csv, row.names = FALSE)
  message(sprintf("Wrote summary CSV: %s", eval_csv))
}

```

# 6 - Per outcome exploration
```{r}
# =========================================================
# EXPLORATION / THRESHOLD ANALYSIS FUNCTIONS
# These power the per-outcome exploration PDFs below.
# =========================================================

# --- Basic cleaning: add numeric significance flag, coerce types ---
clean_gam_summary <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      significant     = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Significant", "Not Significant"),
      significant_num = ifelse(significant == "Significant", 1, 0),
      significant_f   = factor(significant_num,
                               levels = c(0, 1),
                               labels = c("Not Significant", "Significant")),
      range    = as.numeric(range),
      n        = as.numeric(n),
      variable = as.character(variable),
      id       = as.character(id)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(range), !is.na(significant_num),
                  !is.na(variable), !is.na(id))
}

# --- Filter to one outcome variable, one row per ID ---
# Aggregates across potential multiple time_var rows per id/variable.
# peak_time, trough_time, and best_time_var come from the row with the
# smallest p.value (best significance), ensuring internal consistency.
get_outcome_df <- function(df, target_var) {
  df %>%
    dplyr::filter(variable == target_var) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(id, variable) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      p.value         = min(p.value, na.rm = TRUE),
      range           = max(range,   na.rm = TRUE),
      n               = max(n,       na.rm = TRUE),
      significant_num = ifelse(any(significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE), 1, 0),
      # Take timing from the most-significant (smallest p) time_var row
      peak_time       = peak_time[which.min(p.value)][1],
      trough_time     = trough_time[which.min(p.value)][1],
      best_time_var   = time_var[which.min(p.value)][1],
      .groups         = "drop"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      significant_f = factor(
        significant_num,
        levels = c(0, 1),
        labels = c("Not Significant", "Significant")
      )
    )
}

# --- Descriptive summary (overall + by significance) ---
summarize_cyclicity <- function(df_outcome) {
  overall <- df_outcome %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      n_participants   = dplyr::n(),
      n_significant    = sum(significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = mean(significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  by_sig <- df_outcome %>%
    dplyr::group_by(significant_f) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      n            = dplyr::n(),
      mean_range   = mean(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_range     = sd(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_range = median(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      q25_range    = quantile(range, .25, na.rm = TRUE),
      q75_range    = quantile(range, .75, na.rm = TRUE),
      min_range    = min(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      max_range    = max(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_n       = mean(n, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups      = "drop"
    )
  list(overall = overall, by_significance = by_sig)
}

# --- Proportion significant at various range thresholds ---
threshold_table <- function(df_outcome,
                            thresholds = c(0.10, 0.25, 0.36, 0.50, 0.75, 1.00)) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(thresholds, function(th) {
    tmp <- df_outcome %>% dplyr::filter(range >= th)
    tibble::tibble(
      threshold        = th,
      n_at_or_above    = nrow(tmp),
      prop_of_sample   = ifelse(nrow(df_outcome) > 0,
                                nrow(tmp) / nrow(df_outcome), NA_real_),
      n_significant    = sum(tmp$significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = ifelse(nrow(tmp) > 0,
                                mean(tmp$significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
                                NA_real_)
    )
  }))
}

# --- Logistic regression: significance ~ range (& + n) ---
fit_logistic_models <- function(df_outcome) {
  model_range   <- glm(significant_num ~ range,     family = binomial, data = df_outcome)
  model_range_n <- glm(significant_num ~ range + n, family = binomial, data = df_outcome)
  list(
    model_range   = model_range,
    model_range_n = model_range_n,
    tidy_range    = broom::tidy(model_range,   exponentiate = TRUE),
    tidy_range_n  = broom::tidy(model_range_n, exponentiate = TRUE)
  )
}

# --- Predicted probability of significance across range values ---
make_prediction_df <- function(df_outcome, model_range, model_range_n = NULL,
                               n_value = NULL) {
  xseq <- seq(min(df_outcome$range, na.rm = TRUE),
              max(df_outcome$range, na.rm = TRUE),
              length.out = 300)
  pred1 <- tibble::tibble(range = xseq) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pred_prob = predict(model_range, newdata = ., type = "response"),
                  model     = "Range only")
  if (!is.null(model_range_n)) {
    if (is.null(n_value)) n_value <- median(df_outcome$n, na.rm = TRUE)
    pred2 <- tibble::tibble(range = xseq, n = n_value) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(pred_prob = predict(model_range_n, newdata = ., type = "response"),
                    model     = paste0("Range + n (n fixed at ", round(n_value, 1), ")")) %>%
      dplyr::select(range, pred_prob, model)
    dplyr::bind_rows(pred1, pred2)
  } else {
    pred1
  }
}

# --- Histogram of range, colored by significance ---
plot_range_histogram <- function(df_outcome) {
  ggplot2::ggplot(df_outcome, ggplot2::aes(x = range, fill = significant_f)) +
    ggplot2::geom_histogram(position = "identity", alpha = 0.65, bins = 30) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste0("Histogram of ", unique(df_outcome$variable), " Range by Significance"),
      x = "Cyclical range", y = "Count", fill = NULL
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
}

# --- Logistic curve with jittered observed points ---
plot_significance_probability <- function(df_outcome, pred_df,
                                          thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50),
                                          jitter_height = 0.04) {
  ggplot2::ggplot(df_outcome, ggplot2::aes(x = range, y = significant_num)) +
    ggplot2::geom_jitter(ggplot2::aes(color = significant_f),
                         width = 0, height = jitter_height,
                         alpha = 0.45, size = 2) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(data = pred_df,
                       ggplot2::aes(x = range, y = pred_prob, linetype = model),
                       linewidth = 1.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = thresholds, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.02, 1.02), breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),
                                labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste0("Probability of Significant Cyclicity: ", unique(df_outcome$variable)),
      x = "Cyclical range",
      y = "Predicted probability of significant GAM",
      color = NULL, linetype = NULL
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
}

# --- Binned empirical proportions ---
make_empirical_bins <- function(df_outcome, n_bins = 10) {
  df_outcome %>%
    dplyr::mutate(range_bin = dplyr::ntile(range, n_bins)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(range_bin) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      mean_range       = mean(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = mean(significant_num, na.rm = TRUE),
      n                = dplyr::n(),
      .groups          = "drop"
    )
}

# --- Empirical bins + model overlay ---
plot_empirical_and_model <- function(df_outcome, pred_df, empirical_df,
                                     thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50)) {
  ggplot2::ggplot() +
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = empirical_df,
                        ggplot2::aes(x = mean_range, y = prop_significant, size = n),
                        alpha = 0.8) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(data = pred_df,
                       ggplot2::aes(x = range, y = pred_prob, linetype = model),
                       linewidth = 1.2) +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = thresholds, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1),
                                labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title    = paste0("Empirical and Modeled Probability: ", unique(df_outcome$variable)),
      x        = "Cyclical range",
      y        = "Probability of significant GAM",
      size     = "Bin n",
      linetype = NULL
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
}

# --- All-in-one analysis wrapper for one outcome variable ---
analyze_one_outcome <- function(df, target_var,
                                thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50),
                                n_bins = 10) {
  df_clean   <- clean_gam_summary(df)
  df_outcome <- get_outcome_df(df_clean, target_var)
  if (nrow(df_outcome) == 0) stop(paste("No rows found for variable:", target_var))

  desc      <- summarize_cyclicity(df_outcome)
  thresh_tab <- threshold_table(df_outcome, thresholds = thresholds)
  mods      <- fit_logistic_models(df_outcome)
  pred_df   <- make_prediction_df(df_outcome  = df_outcome,
                                  model_range   = mods$model_range,
                                  model_range_n = mods$model_range_n)
  emp_df    <- make_empirical_bins(df_outcome, n_bins = n_bins)

  p_hist <- plot_range_histogram(df_outcome)
  p_prob <- plot_significance_probability(df_outcome, pred_df, thresholds = thresholds)
  p_emp  <- plot_empirical_and_model(df_outcome, pred_df, emp_df, thresholds = thresholds)

  list(
    data                        = df_outcome,
    descriptives_overall        = desc$overall,
    descriptives_by_significance = desc$by_significance,
    threshold_table             = thresh_tab,
    model_range                 = mods$model_range,
    model_range_n               = mods$model_range_n,
    model_range_tidy_OR         = mods$tidy_range,
    model_range_n_tidy_OR       = mods$tidy_range_n,
    predictions                 = pred_df,
    empirical_bins              = emp_df,
    plot_histogram              = p_hist,
    plot_probability            = p_prob,
    plot_empirical              = p_emp
  )
}

# --- Extract DRSP variable names from the summary data ---
get_drsp_variables <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    clean_gam_summary() %>%
    dplyr::distinct(variable) %>%
    dplyr::pull(variable) %>%
    .[grepl("^drsp", ., ignore.case = TRUE)]
}

# --- Extract ADHD symptom variable names (CSS items) from the summary data ---
get_adhd_variables <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    clean_gam_summary() %>%
    dplyr::distinct(variable) %>%
    dplyr::pull(variable) %>%
    .[grepl("^CSS_", .)]
}

# --- Peak and trough timing distribution plot for significant participants ---
plot_peak_trough_timing <- function(df_sig, tv, axis_meta_list, outcome_label) {
  meta <- axis_meta_list[[tv]]

  tl_peak   <- df_sig %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(peak_time)) %>%
    dplyr::transmute(time = peak_time, type = "Peak (Max)")
  tl_trough <- df_sig %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(trough_time)) %>%
    dplyr::transmute(time = trough_time, type = "Trough (Min)")

  # Need >= 2 observations per group for density estimation
  if (nrow(tl_peak) < 2 || nrow(tl_trough) < 2) return(NULL)

  tl_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(tl_peak, tl_trough)

  # Peaks go upward; troughs are mirrored downward so lower symptoms look lower
  ggplot2::ggplot() +
    ggplot2::geom_density(data = tl_peak,
                          ggplot2::aes(x = time,
                                       y = ggplot2::after_stat(ndensity),
                                       fill = "Peak (Max)", color = "Peak (Max)"),
                          alpha = 0.3, adjust = 1.2) +
    ggplot2::geom_density(data = tl_trough,
                          ggplot2::aes(x = time,
                                       y = -ggplot2::after_stat(ndensity),
                                       fill = "Trough (Min)", color = "Trough (Min)"),
                          alpha = 0.3, adjust = 1.2) +
    ggplot2::geom_rug(data = tl_all, ggplot2::aes(x = time, color = type),
                      alpha = 0.5, length = grid::unit(0.03, "npc")) +
    ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "grey40") +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$menses, color = "red", linewidth = 0.8,
                        linetype = "dashed") +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$ov, color = "forestgreen", linewidth = 0.8,
                        linetype = "dashed") +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1), breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.5),
                                labels = meta$labels) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1),
                                breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.25),
                                labels = abs) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste("Peak & Trough Timing:", outcome_label),
      subtitle = paste0("Among participants with significant GAMs (p < .05); N = ",
                        nrow(df_sig)),
      x = "Cycle Phase",
      y = "Peaks \u2191 / Troughs \u2193 (normalized density)",
      fill = NULL, color = NULL
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = c("Peak (Max)" = "#D55E00",
                                          "Trough (Min)" = "#0072B2")) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = c("Peak (Max)" = "#D55E00",
                                           "Trough (Min)" = "#0072B2"))
}
```

# 7 - Per outcome reports
```{r}
# =========================================================
# GENERATE PER-OUTCOME EXPLORATION PDF REPORTS
# =========================================================
# For each DRSP outcome, generates a landscape PDF containing:
#   1. Summary page: descriptive stats + threshold table + peak/trough timing
#   2. Significance probability plot (logistic curve)
#   3. Histogram of range by significance
#   4. Empirical + modeled probability plot
#   Per time variable:
#   5. Peak & trough timing density (significant participants)
#   6+. Individual GAM trajectory pages (fixed Y axes across people):
#       - Significant with range > 0.33 first, then
#       - Significant with range <= 0.33, then
#       - Not significant with range > 0.33
# =========================================================

# --- Build the exploration summary from in-memory results ---
if (length(all_ids_signif) > 0) {
  gam_summary_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(all_ids_signif)

  # Create output sub-folder for exploration PDFs
  explore_folder <- file.path(output_folder, "Outcome_Exploration_PDFs")
  dir.create(explore_folder, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

  drsp_vars <- get_drsp_variables(gam_summary_df)
  adhd_vars <- get_adhd_variables(gam_summary_df)
  all_outcome_vars <- c(adhd_vars, drsp_vars)
  cat("Found", length(adhd_vars), "ADHD symptom (CSS) variables and",
      length(drsp_vars), "DRSP variables to process\n")

  # Thresholds for the exploration
  explore_thresholds <- c(0.10, 0.20, 0.25, 0.36, 0.50, 0.75, 1.00)

  # Plot layout for individual GAM pages
  plots_per_col_exp <- 3
  plots_per_row_exp <- 3
  max_plots_per_page_exp <- plots_per_col_exp * plots_per_row_exp

  # Table themes for summary pages
  summary_table_theme <- gridExtra::ttheme_default(
    base_size = 11,
    core    = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0, x = 0.05)),
    colhead = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0, x = 0.05, fontface = "bold"))
  )
  threshold_table_theme <- gridExtra::ttheme_default(
    base_size = 10,
    core    = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5)),
    colhead = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5, fontface = "bold"))
  )

  for (target_var in all_outcome_vars) {
    cat(sprintf("\n=== Processing: %s ===\n", target_var))

    # 1. Run exploration analysis
    result <- tryCatch(
      analyze_one_outcome(gam_summary_df, target_var,
                          thresholds = explore_thresholds,
                          n_bins = 10),
      error = function(e) {
        message("  Skipping ", target_var, ": ", e$message)
        NULL
      }
    )
    if (is.null(result)) next

    # 2. Identify qualifying IDs in four groups, each sorted by range descending:
    #    (a) Significant & range > 0.33
    #    (b) Significant & range <= 0.33
    #    (c) Not significant & range > 0.33
    #    (d) Not significant & range <= 0.33
    sig_above_cutoff <- result$data %>%
      dplyr::filter(significant_num == 1 & range > 0.33) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(range)) %>%
      dplyr::pull(id)

    sig_below_cutoff <- result$data %>%
      dplyr::filter(significant_num == 1 & range <= 0.33) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(range)) %>%
      dplyr::pull(id)

    nonsig_above_cutoff <- result$data %>%
      dplyr::filter(significant_num == 0 & range > 0.33) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(range)) %>%
      dplyr::pull(id)

    nonsig_below_cutoff <- result$data %>%
      dplyr::filter(significant_num == 0 & range <= 0.33) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(range)) %>%
      dplyr::pull(id)

    qualifying_ids <- c(sig_above_cutoff, sig_below_cutoff,
                        nonsig_above_cutoff, nonsig_below_cutoff)

    # 3. Get outcome label
    outcome_label <- if (target_var %in% names(outcomes)) {
      outcomes[[target_var]]
    } else {
      target_var
    }

    cat(sprintf("  N participants: %d | N significant: %d | N qualifying IDs: %d\n",
                result$descriptives_overall$n_participants,
                result$descriptives_overall$n_significant,
                length(qualifying_ids)))

    # 4. Open PDF
    pdf_path <- file.path(explore_folder, paste0("Explore_", target_var, ".pdf"))
    grDevices::pdf(pdf_path, width = 11, height = 8.5)

    # ---- PAGE 1: Summary + Threshold Table ----
    title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
      paste("GAM Cyclicity Exploration:", outcome_label),
      gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold")
    )
    subtitle_grob <- grid::textGrob(
      paste("Variable:", target_var),
      gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "italic")
    )

    n_cpass <- sum(result$data$significant_num == 1 &
                    is.finite(result$data$range) &
                    result$data$range >= 1.5, na.rm = TRUE)

    n_total <- result$descriptives_overall$n_participants
    pct_cpass <- if (n_total > 0) n_cpass / n_total * 100 else NA_real_

    desc_df <- data.frame(
      Metric = c("Total Participants",
                 "N Significant (p < .05)",
                 "Proportion Significant",
                 "N Range \u22651.5 (p < .05 & range \u2265 1.5)",
                 "% Range \u22651.5 (of total sample)",
                 "Median Range",
                 "Mean Observations per ID"),
      Value = c(
        as.character(n_total),
        as.character(result$descriptives_overall$n_significant),
        sprintf("%.1f%%", result$descriptives_overall$prop_significant * 100),
        as.character(n_cpass),
        ifelse(is.na(pct_cpass), "N/A", sprintf("%.1f%%", pct_cpass)),
        sprintf("%.2f", median(result$data$range, na.rm = TRUE)),
        sprintf("%.1f", mean(result$data$n, na.rm = TRUE))
      ),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )

    # Add peak/trough timing summary for significant participants, per time_var
    sig_peak_data <- result$data %>%
      dplyr::filter(significant_num == 1, !is.na(peak_time), !is.na(trough_time))

    if (nrow(sig_peak_data) > 0) {
      timing_by_tv <- sig_peak_data %>%
        dplyr::group_by(best_time_var) %>%
        dplyr::summarise(
          med_peak   = median(peak_time, na.rm = TRUE),
          med_trough = median(trough_time, na.rm = TRUE),
          n_sig      = dplyr::n(),
          .groups    = "drop"
        )
      for (i in seq_len(nrow(timing_by_tv))) {
        tv_label <- timing_by_tv$best_time_var[i]
        suffix <- if (nrow(timing_by_tv) > 1) paste0(" [", tv_label, "]") else ""
        desc_df <- rbind(desc_df, data.frame(
          Metric = c(paste0("Median Peak Time (sig.)", suffix),
                      paste0("Median Trough Time (sig.)", suffix)),
          Value = c(
            sprintf("%.2f (N=%d)", timing_by_tv$med_peak[i], timing_by_tv$n_sig[i]),
            sprintf("%.2f (N=%d)", timing_by_tv$med_trough[i], timing_by_tv$n_sig[i])
          ),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
      }
    }

    desc_tbl <- gridExtra::tableGrob(desc_df, rows = NULL, theme = summary_table_theme)

    thresh_display <- result$threshold_table %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        threshold        = sprintf("%.2f", threshold),
        n_at_or_above    = as.character(n_at_or_above),
        prop_of_sample   = sprintf("%.1f%%", prop_of_sample * 100),
        n_significant    = as.character(n_significant),
        prop_significant = ifelse(is.na(prop_significant), "N/A",
                                  sprintf("%.1f%%", prop_significant * 100))
      )
    names(thresh_display) <- c("Range Threshold", "N >= Threshold",
                               "% of Sample", "N Significant", "% Significant")

    thresh_tbl <- gridExtra::tableGrob(thresh_display, rows = NULL,
                                       theme = threshold_table_theme)
    thresh_header <- grid::textGrob(
      "Significance Rates at Range Thresholds",
      gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold")
    )
    note_grob <- if (length(qualifying_ids) > 0) {
      grid::textGrob(
        sprintf("Individual GAM pages follow for %d IDs (sig range>0.33, sig range\u22640.33, non-sig range>0.33, non-sig range\u22640.33)",
                length(qualifying_ids)),
        gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11, fontface = "italic")
      )
    } else {
      grid::textGrob(
        "No qualifying IDs for individual GAM pages",
        gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11, fontface = "italic")
      )
    }

    gridExtra::grid.arrange(
      title_grob, subtitle_grob, desc_tbl, thresh_header, thresh_tbl, note_grob,
      ncol = 1,
      # Heights: title, subtitle, desc table, thresh header, thresh table, note
      heights = grid::unit(c(0.10, 0.05, 0.25, 0.08, 0.38, 0.08), "npc")
    )

    # ---- PAGE 2: Significance Probability Plot ----
    print(result$plot_probability)

    # ---- PAGE 3: Histogram ----
    print(result$plot_histogram)

    # ---- PAGE 4: Empirical + Model ----
    print(result$plot_empirical)

    # ---- PAGE 5+: Peak & Trough Timing Summary (per time variable) ----
    for (tv in time_vars) {
      sig_timing_data <- result$data %>%
        dplyr::filter(significant_num == 1, best_time_var == tv,
                      !is.na(peak_time), !is.na(trough_time))

      if (nrow(sig_timing_data) > 0) {
        p_timing <- plot_peak_trough_timing(sig_timing_data, tv, axis_meta,
                                            outcome_label)
        if (!is.null(p_timing)) print(p_timing)
      }
    }

    # ---- Individual GAM trajectory plots for all IDs (per time variable) ----
    # Pre-compute predictions for global y-axis; groups: sig>0.33, sig<=0.33, nonsig>0.33, nonsig<=0.33
    for (tv in time_vars) {
      if (length(qualifying_ids) == 0) next

      # Pre-compute all predictions for global y-axis range
      all_fit_results <- list()
      for (qid in qualifying_ids) {
        dat_id <- subset(cycle_df_scaled, as.character(id) == qid)
        if (nrow(dat_id) == 0) next

        res <- tryCatch(
          fit_outcome(dat_id, target_var, tv, outcomes),
          error = function(e) NULL
        )
        if (!is.null(res)) all_fit_results[[as.character(qid)]] <- res
      }

      groups <- list(
        list(ids = sig_above_cutoff,
             label = "Significant, Range > 0.33"),
        list(ids = sig_below_cutoff,
             label = "Significant, Range \u2264 0.33"),
        list(ids = nonsig_above_cutoff,
             label = "Not Significant, Range > 0.33"),
        list(ids = nonsig_below_cutoff,
             label = "Not Significant, Range \u2264 0.33")
      )

      for (grp in groups) {
        if (length(grp$ids) == 0) next

        id_plots <- list()
        for (qid in grp$ids) {
          res <- all_fit_results[[as.character(qid)]]
          if (is.null(res)) next

          p <- create_annotated_plot(res$pred, tv, axis_meta)
          if (!is.null(p)) {
            id_range <- round(res$sig$range, 2)
            id_n     <- res$sig$n
            id_pval  <- res$sig$p.value
            id_stars <- dplyr::case_when(
              is.finite(id_pval) & id_pval < 0.001 ~ "***",
              is.finite(id_pval) & id_pval < 0.01  ~ "**",
              is.finite(id_pval) & id_pval < 0.05  ~ "*",
              TRUE ~ ""
            )
            p <- p + ggplot2::labs(
              title = sprintf("ID %s %s (range=%.2f, N=%d)", qid, id_stars, id_range, id_n)
            )
            # Red title when either phase has fewer than 5 observations
            if (isTRUE(res$sig$n_pre < 5) || isTRUE(res$sig$n_post < 5)) {
              p <- p + ggplot2::theme(
                plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10, face = "bold",
                                                   hjust = 0.5, color = "red")
              )
            }
            id_plots[[length(id_plots) + 1]] <- p
          }
        }

        if (length(id_plots) > 0) {
          pages <- split(id_plots,
                         ceiling(seq_along(id_plots) / max_plots_per_page_exp))

          for (page_plots in pages) {
            if (length(page_plots) < max_plots_per_page_exp) {
              page_plots <- c(page_plots,
                             rep(list(patchwork::plot_spacer()),
                                 max_plots_per_page_exp - length(page_plots)))
            }
            final_page <- patchwork::wrap_plots(plotlist = page_plots,
                                                ncol = plots_per_col_exp) +
              patchwork::plot_annotation(
                title    = paste(grp$label, ":", outcome_label),
                subtitle = paste("Time variable:", tv),
                theme    = ggplot2::theme(
                  plot.title    = ggplot2::element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
                  plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11)
                )
              )
            print(final_page)
          }
        }
      }
    }

    grDevices::dev.off()
    message(sprintf("  Saved: %s", pdf_path))
  }

  cat("\n=== All exploration PDFs generated ===\n")
  cat("Output folder:", explore_folder, "\n")
} else {
  message("No significance data available; skipping per-outcome exploration PDFs.")
}
```
